title: Effects of adaptation to altered color statistics provide evidence for calibration of color perception to the color statistics of natural scenes.

title: Visual working memory for real-world objects is resistant to visual but vulnerable to semantic interference.

It also examines whether informational complexity or utility uncertainty drives behavioral differences.

title: Reference points, risk-taking behavior, and competitive outcomes in sequential settings
llm_reason: The paper examines individual-level risk-taking decisions under competitive pressure in a sequential setting, which directly relates to how people evaluate and respond to reference points and uncertainty. The heterogeneity in responses and the role of reference points connect to core topics like loss aversion and prospect theory at the individual level.

title: Uncertainty (ambiguity) aversion and seeking
llm_reason: Ambiguity aversion and seeking are core topics in individual decision making under uncertainty, directly addressing how people evaluate options with unknown probabilities—a central concern of cognitive and process-oriented research on choice.

title: Learning from the storm: The impact of hurricane experience on future disaster preparedness
llm_reason: The paper focuses on field-level household purchasing behavior in response to hurricane experience, without modeling individual cognitive processes, attention, or decision mechanisms. It is primarily an econometric study of behavioral outcomes rather than a cognitive or process-oriented account of decision making under risk.

title: Different sources of the numerical comparison size effect
llm_reason: The paper directly investigates decision processes in numerical comparison tasks using a diffusion model framework, examining decision thresholds, response times, and cognitive mechanisms—core process-level concerns relevant to individual decision making and evidence accumulation models.
